Out of gratitude that we are receiving so much, the minimum what we can do is express our gratitude, be grateful, have an attitude of gratitude, and the natural consequence of that gratitude would be, let me render some service, devotional service.
Because the Lord is so great, so kind, so merciful, so magnanimous, He is giving me so many things, let me do something for Him, some menial service for Him.
Only an ungrateful soul, a person who does not recognize, will wonder why I should do anything in return.
Even as disciples.
As devotees, we have to be very, very careful that sometimes we can take all the blessings what we have for granted.
We are getting nice prasadam, we are getting an opportunity to associate with devotees, we are staying in the ashram, the atmosphere is so congenial for spiritual growth.
We have regular classes, morning till 9.30, we are all involved in various spiritual practices.
Now, after this, we will go, we will have prasadam.
Prasadam is ready.
So we can take this for granted, and sometimes we can even start cribbing, or so to say complaining about small, small things, lacunas we might find in our lives, in the system.
In fact, by default, as conditioned souls, we are ungrateful.
Conditioned soul means that natural, loving attitude, loving service to the Lord is covered.
Jivera swaroopo hai nityera krishna das.
That attitude, somehow, because of contamination, because of conditioning, because of having overpowered by the lower modes, rajo guna and tamo guna, that's covered.
Sometimes we even start rebelling.
And demanding, and even complaining about injustice.
So as a natural, we are conditioned to be ungrateful.
That is conditional life.
That's why it says, what grateful soul is there who would not render devotional service?
So even as devotees, we have to be very, very careful.
Even sometimes, we can pause for a while, sit somewhere.
And jot down, what are the wonderful things in our life, for which we have to be grateful for.
Naturally, the mind, Krishna says, mind if it is uncontrolled is the worst.
By default, it goes on what is not okay.
What's wrong?
What bad thing has happened to me?
What injustice has happened to me?
That is the default mode in which the mind works.
What good is happening to us?
Even karmas have realized that the attitude of gratitude is very, very important if a
person has to be happy, and successful in life.
You can see the glass half empty, you can also see the glass half filled.
It's not what's there in the glass.
It's what's there in your mind.
What's there in your vision?
What's your thinking?
So as devotees,

But as devotees also it is very important, naturally there will be some injustice, naturally there will be some lacunae in the system, some wrongs happening to us, according to kala, karma, things will unfold.
It depends where we put our focus on.
Sometimes even consciously we can sit down and jot down all the things which are happening in my life, which are blessings and benedictions from the Almighty Lord.

Even as devotees we get prasadam, we have the holy name to chant, we have association of devotees, we have an opportunity to have darshan of the Lord, we have to have opportunity to see beautiful alankaras.
We participate in kirtans, we have tirtha, we smell the flowers which is offered to the Lord, we drink the water which has been offered to the Lord.
All these things many times taken for granted.
Another thing when we live here in association of devotees, all of us have our assigned services.
It is important that we do our services very very sincerely.
Sometimes again because of conditioning we are lazy, we are reluctant, we shrug off from the services.
We are enjoying the services of other devotees, just like now after the Bhagavatam class we will go, the prasadam will be ready.
The devotees will be always pushing the Neo-Bhagavatam one way or another...
Very expensive to visit them at that time, who are given the service and are porridge,
the prasadam will be ready, hot, cooked, offered.
Just to sit down and have prasadam.
This is a service in the material world, it is a give and take.
You take some service and then you have to pay in return.
. Just like I gave the example of Masτι's.ve.
They gave example of a market. You go to a shop, you buy something, you take something and you give in return. You give your money in return.
So likewise, here also, we enjoy the service of other devotees and in return we have to do our assigned services.
And if we shrug from our assigned services or don't do our assigned services or don't do our services sincerely,
in that sense also, we will not be happy because Prabhupada here says he is called a thief or a rogue.
One who enjoys these things, gifts from the Lord, without feeling grateful and without giving in return, he is called a thief and a rogue.
In the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna mentions about the five truths, Jeeva, Prakriti, Kala, Karma and Ishvara.
And of all these things, all are eternal except for karma.
Prabhupada explains why karma is not eternal because we act and based on our actions, we have some reactions.
And all the Jeevas, if you see in the material world, we are caught up in a web or mesh of actions and reactions.
We desire, to fulfill that desire, we act, act independent of the Lord, completely oblivious of the presence of the Lord, forgetful of the Lord.
We want to enjoy independently and then we act, we desire, based on that we create karmas, based on that there are reactions.
And then we enjoy or suffer those reactions and further it creates the reactions, it's like an endless cycle, it's like a bhava, it's like a whirlwind.
Rather than wings or threads, we think it is this simple, but we filled it with religious
bribery.

protector of the verfieckpaul. To give an example, let's say a butcher, already he's sinful based on that he has got the life of a butcher and he is committing more sins. In that life he's committing more sin. It's like a negative spiral going down and now in a material by a large people are in the negative spiral. Already there is sinful based on that they have got the bodies and in that the body devoid of Krishna consciousness with no knowledge of the worship of the Divinest being deivy 2.
service, they are further getting entangled. And based on that entanglement, based on desires
which are unfulfilled, that jiva is getting life after life different bodies. And it's
an endless cycle. Why karma is called, is not eternal? Because by proper guidance, by
culture of Krishna consciousness, the actions and the subsequent reactions can be changed.
We act because of false ego in conditional stage. We have different desires. We have
misgivings, I am the body, I am the enjoyer, we completely forgetful of the Lord. By culture
In the nature of Krishna consciousness we can purify those desires, do the right kind of actions, and liberate ourselves from this material existence.
What are right actions? Right actions are nothing but devotional service. Purified actions are nothing but devotional service.

Jīvera svarūpa hoye nityara kṛṣṇa dāsa. That's our svarūpa, purified state. Only in contaminated stage we are forgetful of Lord. Only in a contaminated condition stage we think that we are the enjoyer.
When you remove that contamination, the natural what remains is to serve the Lord.
So the whole process of bhakti yoga, what we are following, is to remove this contamination.
To come to a purified stage. Purified stage means there is a gratitude. There is a recognition that, yes, I am blessed by so many things. The Lord is my master. He is giving me so many things.
And when there is recognition, when there is gratitude, when there is dependence on the Lord, just like you are saying, we will be completely exclusively dependent on the Lord.
Right now we have made so many arrangements for our protection. Why we are not dependent on the Lord? Why we are forgetful of the Lord?
Because we have faith in our own abilities, own mechanisms, own protection, safeguards, what we have built.
The more and more we get freed from that conditioning, we become purified, the more and more we exclusively depend on the Lord.
We have faith in the Lord's protection. We have the conviction that Lord is the master. I am a servant.
And by devotional service, by practice of bhakti yoga, we can get out of this conditioning.
One time it so happened that there was a dilapidated hut, and in that hut there were many rat holes.
It was on a bank of a river. And the rats would, in the daytime, come out of those holes and move around.
Sometimes go to nearby shops, collect those items, whatever they like to eat, and then come back.
It so happened that one day it rained very heavily, poured very heavily,
just like we had that recently in Shardham.
So the river got inundated, started overflowing, and the water entered that hut, inundated the entire hut,
and all the rat holes were filled with water.
Those rats somehow tried surviving, but since they were caught unaware,
and practically all the rat holes were filled with water, all of them died.
Luckily, while all this havoc was happening, on that river there was one log of wood,
big, huge log of wood that was going downstream.
And one of the rats, which was struggling somehow to remain afloat and survive,
got pushed by a wave and landed upon that log.
While there was, water was gushing through and,
the whole place was getting inundated with water, but somehow this log was floating,
and this rat, completely drenched, was sitting on top of the log, flowing downstream.
And luckily this log striked some bank, and this mouse, which was sitting on top of the log,
got somehow pushed onto the land and saved himself, got saved.
This is a very crude example, there is no one-on-one analogy,
but nevertheless, to drive home the point, there is some similarity to what is our condition.
We are living in this material world as compared to this dilapidated hut.
And the floods, the samsara sagara,
when kala unfolds various miseries, we can get completely inundated.
Kala will flow and even people, mirtyu haras sarvacaham, Krishna says,
everything will be destroyed, everything will end in destruction.
We have a good body, as for six transformations, slowly it will decay and eventually die.
So everyone is getting so-called inundated and being killed, mirtyu haras sarvacaham, Krishna says.
But this log is compared, just like it says, we hear this word,
the captain of the ship,
the ship in which,
the spiritual master is the captain.
We can get into that boat.
When a person is drowning, he cannot save himself just by swimming nicely.
We require an external help.
We require someone who can come and pick us up.
And sometimes it can be out of compassion.
Sometimes when tragedies happen, a lot of people who have that seva bhava,
who want to help people, they go to such areas.
They rush to these areas on humanitarian grounds to save people.
So Lord is very magnanimous, very merciful,
just like here we saw that he came to protect Gajendra.
He himself comes and sends his pure devotees to pick up,
to reclaim the conditioned soul which is being floored by Samsara Sagara,
inundated in Samsara Sagara.
And this rat somehow got saved, swimming on that log.
If we take shelter of a pure devotee,
take shelter of his lotus feet,
even though there is destruction all around us,
birth, death, punarapi janma, punarapi marna,
the Samsara Sagara, we are also in that Sagara,
we can get saved.
That is what the process of devotional service is.
Get into the boat,
which is, so to say,
led by the spiritual master,
an expert sailor,
who can take us to the right destination.
And what is the destination?
Back to our original home, the kingdom of God.
And if we don't take shelter of devotional service,
don't take shelter of a pure devotee of the Lord,
just like those rats,
we'll all be killed, vanquished,
by Kala.
And again, based on our desires,
again, punarapi janma,
punarapi marna,
that goes on.
So what is the root cause of our material existence?
In one lecture it was being mentioned that how
the living entity is there,
and then we have this body made of
the senses, the mind, intelligence.
In the pure state,
purified intelligence, the purified mind and the senses,
engage in the service of the Lord,
devotional service.
The senses, eyes, see the beautiful form of the Lord.
Ears have a natural inclination to hear about the Lord,
hear the glories of the Lord,
hear kirtans, hear the holy name.
Nose has a natural inclination to smell something aromatic.
The lotus feet of the Lord,
the flowers which are offered to the Lord,
the incense which is offered to the Lord,
you know, we have the sticks which are offered to the Lord.
So in a normal situation,
the soul is there,
the intelligence is there,
the mind is there and the senses are there.
And they all are acting
in purified stage
to serve the Lord.
The intelligence is
making different plans
how to serve the Lord better.
The mind,
which is the principal sense,
controller of all the senses,
is also engaged
in dictating the senses
to serve the Lord,
to glorify the Lord.
Just like we have an example of Ambarish Maharaj.
This is a normal stage.
In a conditioned stage what happens is,
it is important for us to understand,
there comes a plunderer
between the soul
and the intelligence.
What is the name of that plunderer?
That plunderer is called the false ego.
The false ego,
the main function is identification,
desires.
Ego is just like saying,
what is ego?
Your identity.
False ego means wrong identity.
False ego plunderer means
identity that I am the enjoyer.
I am this body.
And when this false ego comes,
then the intelligence picks up the desire
from this ego, false ego,
gives that to the mind,
mind to the senses,
and there is havoc in the system.
The senses which are meant to serve the Lord
start acting
in a way which is detrimental
to the self-interest of the soul.
The intelligence becomes contaminated.
The mind becomes uncontrolled.
The senses become untamed.
This is our present state.
This is the meaning of conditioned life.
The root cause is what?
The false ego.
What is the false ego?
I am the enjoyer.
There is no Lord.
I have to take care of myself.
So through the process of devotion,
what is happening is,
slowly our false ego
is getting transformed into real ego.
What is real ego?
Jivera Swaroopa Hohe Nityera Krishna Das
Krishna is the real enjoyer.
He is the master.
Everything what I have,
my abilities, my skills, my talents,
is coming from the Lord.
In a purified sense,
naturally that feeling of gratitude,
that gratitude is there.
In that stage where we are overpowered
by false ego,
naturally we will be rebellious.
We will always be ungrateful.
There are different shades of false ego.
You can be grateful in a material sense,
but still be ungrateful to the Lord.
Genuine gratefulness is,
we are grateful to the Absolute.
We recognize that He is the cause of all causes.
You can be grateful up to the level of
grateful to the parents,
grateful to the teacher,
grateful to the society,
grateful to the government.
But still you are not absolutely grateful
because you don't recognize
that the parents are instrumental
in giving what is due to you.
So slowly,
through the process of devotional service,
by doing devotional service,
by the process of bhakti yoga,
the false ego gets purified.
And when the false ego gets purified,
we do more bhakti yoga.
Because we do more bhakti yoga,
the false ego gets purified.
And because of purification of false ego,
we do more devotional activities.
This is a positive spiral.
So the moment we take up
the process of devotional service,
we start becoming devotees,
what we are getting into
is a positive spiral.
What is positive spiral?
We have false ego.
We engage in devotional service.
We hear, chant, glorify.
It purifies the ego.
Because the ego gets purified,
it could be temporarily.
Because of that purified ego,
we are motivated,
we are inspired.
Just like today,
we are reading in this verse,
what grateful man would be
who would not render devotional service.
We get motivated, inspired
to render devotional service.
Because of that,
the ego gets more purified.
And it goes on in a cyclic manner,
till the point where
the ego is completely purified.
Pure devotee means
completely purified ego,
completely surrendered to the Supreme Lord,
completely thinking of the Lord always.
And the reverse also is possible.
If we lose the association of devotees,
if we stop taking the medicine,
if we stop chanting the holy names,
the false ego becomes thicker and thicker and thicker.
Because of that thick false,
many times we ask people,
why don't you come to the temple?
When he will call, we will come.
The time has to come.
You ask a rich man,
why don't you give some donation?
He has to give, then I will give.
He may have big, big cars
parked in his portico,
in his house.
Lord has to give,
then I will give.
Lot of people have said like that.
Lord has to give.
You tell your Lord to give,
then we will give.
So it's like the dust which is setting on
the false ego,
becoming thicker and thicker and thicker.
So that way we are very grateful to Shri Prabhupada,
we are very blessed.
Although we have false ego,
we are not purified.
Although we are conditioned soul,
we are blessed that we are having a process.
Depends again how well we take the medicine,
how sincerely we follow the process.
The process is there which will purify us
and slowly that good qualities,
gratitude,
rendering devotional service,
understanding that Krishna is my master,
dependence on Krishna,
depending on Krishna for protection,
all those good qualities will start manifesting
in our system.
Stop here.
Granta Raj,
Shrimad Bhagavatam Ki
Dr. Shri Prabhupada Ki
